LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LAW OFFICE OF MAYOR JOSEPH L. ALIOTO, PAMELA PITT AND ANGELA ALIOTO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS (REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE). NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EXHIBITS C THROUGH F, H AND I IS DENIED UNDER EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 452(H) TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANTS SEEK TO HAVE THE CONTENTS OF THE DOCUMENTS ACCEPTED FOR THE TRUTH OF THE MATTER STATED. UNDER CHARNAY V COBERT (2006) 145 CAL. APP. 4TH 170, THE COURT MAY NOT DETERMINE ISSUES OF FACT WHEN RULING ON A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE UNFAIR COMPETITION IS DISMISSED BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S NON-OPPOSITION. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Steven L. Robinson, Esq., for Defendant and moving party: Law Office of Mayor Joseph Alioto

No other appearance stated, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant's motion to compel the deposition of plaintiff is granted. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to scheduling the deposition at a mutually convenient time. The deposition shall be completed by May 21, 2008. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,140 shall be paid on or before May 21, 2008. Commissioner Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn & Mtn To Strike All Or Part Of Complaint, Request For Judicial Ntc
Off Calendar Feb-19-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS LAW OFFICE OF MAYOR JOSEPH L. ALIOTO, PAMELA PITT AND ANGELA ALIOTO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS (REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE).
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST TO TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF EXHIBITS C THROUGH F, H AND I IS DENIED UNDER EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 452(H) TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANTS SEEK TO HAVE THE CONTENTS OF THE DOCUMENTS ACCEPTED FOR THE TRUTH OF THE MATTER STATED. UNDER CHARNAY V COBERT (2006) 145 CAL. APP. 4TH 170, THE COURT MAY NOT DETERMINE ISSUES OF FACT WHEN RULING ON A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE UNFAIR COMPETITION IS DISMISSED BASED ON PLAINTIFF'S NON-OPPOSITION. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
